name: QNI : Email Received From Potentially Hostile Host
notes: This rule triggers when an email flow is received from a host that is known for hostile activities, such as Phishing, Spam, Malware, Botnet Command and Control, or Cryptocurrency Mining.
conditions_text: when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Applications in Flows
when the flow matches Originating User is not N/A

when the flow matches XFORCE_URL_CATEGORY(REPLACEALL('.*\@(.+?)\>?$',"originating user",'$1')) IN ('Phishing URLs', 'Spam URLs', 'Malware', 'Botnet Command and Control Server', 'Cryptocurrency Mining') AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY QNI : Email Received From Potentially Hostile Host on flows which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when a flow matches any of the following BB:CategoryDefinition: Mail Applications in Flows
AND when the flow matches Originating User is not N/A

AND when the flow matches XFORCE_URL_CATEGORY(REPLACEALL('.*\@(.+?)\>?$',"originating user",'$1')) IN ('Phishing URLs', 'Spam URLs', 'Malware', 'Botnet Command and Control Server', 'Cryptocurrency Mining') AQL filter query
RED_list: Event name: QNI : Email Received From Potentially Hostile Host
Event description: Detects when an email flow is received from a host that is known for hostile activities, such as Phishing, Spam, Malware, Botnet Command and Control, or Cryptocurrency Mining.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
